prompt: Summarize the following engineering note in three compact bullets. We are running a quantized mixture-of-experts model on a local machine. The model file is mapped with mmap, and routed expert weights are pulled from NVMe into the operating-system page cache when the CPU kernels touch those pages. The current bottleneck is not pure arithmetic throughput. Most wall time appears to be spent waiting for expert weight pages to become resident. A static hotset prewarm can help cold-start behavior, but it is too blunt for token-by-token decoding because adjacent routed expert sets overlap only partially. The next optimization should separate prefill and decode: prefill can tolerate broader merged reads, while decode needs early and precise routing-aware hints.

prompt: Summarize the following engineering note in three compact bullets. We are running a quantized mixture-of-experts model on a local machine. The model file is mapped with mmap, and routed expert weights are pulled from NVMe into the operating-system page cache when the CPU kernels touch those pages. The current bottleneck is not pure arithmetic throughput. Most wall time appears to be spent waiting for expert weight pages to become resident. A static hotset prewarm can help cold-start behavior, but it is too blunt for token-by-token decoding because adjacent routed expert sets overlap only partially. The next optimization should separate prefill and decode: prefill can tolerate broader merged reads, while decode needs early and precise routing-aware hints.

prompt: Summarize the following engineering note in three compact bullets. We are running a quantized mixture-of-experts model on a local machine. The model file is mapped with mmap, and routed expert weights are pulled from NVMe into the operating-system page cache when the CPU kernels touch those pages. The current bottleneck is not pure arithmetic throughput. Most wall time appears to be spent waiting for expert weight pages to become resident. A static hotset prewarm can help cold-start behavior, but it is too blunt for token-by-token decoding because adjacent routed expert sets overlap only partially. The next optimization should separate prefill and decode: prefill can tolerate broader merged reads, while decode needs early and precise routing-aware hints.

The current bottleneck is not pure arithmetic throughput. Most wall time appears to be spent waiting for expert weight pages to become resident.
